ground_truth:
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

Date: 13/01/2024
Patient: Jennifer Lee  DOB: 14/09/1990  MRN: 882671
Ordering Physician: Dr. Martinez, Neurology

Clinical Indication: Headache, visual disturbance, rule out mass lesion.

Technique:
Multiplanar multisequence MRI of the brain was performed prior to and following administration of 15 mL gadolinium contrast.

Findings:

Parenchymal:
No abnormal signal intensity or enhancement within the brain parenchyma.
No mass, mass effect, or midline shift.
No diffusion restriction to suggest acute infarct.
No susceptibility artifact to suggest hemorrhage.

Ventricles:
Normal size. No hydrocephalus.

Extra-axial Spaces:
No extra-axial fluid collection.

Sella:
Pituitary gland is normal in size and enhancement.

Cerebellopontine Angles:
No enhancing mass.

Meninges:
No abnormal meningeal enhancement.

Vascular:
Major intracranial flow voids are preserved.

Calvarium:
No calvarial abnormality.

Paranasal Sinuses:
Minimal mucosal thickening in the maxillary sinuses bilaterally.

Impression:
1. No intracranial mass, hemorrhage, or acute infarct.
2. Normal MRI brain with contrast.
3. Incidental finding: Mild bilateral maxillary sinus mucosal thickening.

Dr. Patricia Gomez, MD
Neuroradiology
